title: Melted Crunchy Dutch Corn Gratin
description: A roasted, crumbly, and creamy Dutch dish featuring creamed corn, yuzu-cure, and a crunchy topping, perfect for a midnight craving. This ovo-vegetarian-friendly recipe is a twist on traditional Dutch cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and whisk to combine, cooking for 1 minute.
Slowly pour in the milk, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it thickens, stirring occasionally.
Remove the sauce from the heat and stir in the yuzu juice and grated cheese until the cheese is melted.
Add the thawed corn kernels, salt, and pepper to the cheese sauce and stir to combine.
Transfer the corn mixture to a baking dish and top with breadcrumbs.
Beat the egg in a small bowl and brush it over the breadcrumbs to help them brown.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the corn is heated through.
